title: PowerShell Filtering & Selection
description: Learn to filter pipeline output with Where-Object, cmdlet parameters, and Select-String for text search.

tasks.prompt: List processes where CPU usage is greater than 100.
tasks.hint: Pipe to Where-Object with a CPU comparison script block.
tasks.explanation: Where-Object filters objects that satisfy a script-block condition.

tasks.prompt: List only services that are currently running.
tasks.hint: Filter services where Status equals Running.
tasks.explanation: Where-Object supports simplified property comparison syntax.

tasks.prompt: List files in the current directory larger than 1 MB.
tasks.hint: Compare the Length property against 1MB in a script block.
tasks.explanation: PowerShell supports unit suffixes like MB in numeric comparisons.
Many Get-* cmdlets accept filter parameters for targeted retrieval.

tasks.prompt: Retrieve the Windows Update service (wuauserv) by name.
tasks.explanation: Get-Service -Name returns a single service object when the name is known.

tasks.prompt: List only .txt files in the current directory.
tasks.hint: Use Get-ChildItem with -Filter and a wildcard pattern.
tasks.explanation: The -Filter parameter applies a wildcard pattern at the provider level.
-Include works with -Recurse to match files by extension in subfolders.

tasks.prompt: Search 'app.log' for lines containing the word 'error'.
tasks.explanation: Select-String performs pattern matching across file contents like grep.
